publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 54-55. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: My Dearest. I have just written to Kayes desiring a basket of fruit to be sent every other day to the Hen by the Brighton & Windsor coach. This the Hen said would be an accomodation to her.--
The baby woke Green last night by laughing violently
in his sleep. He is most flourishing, & much enjoys a luke-warm salt water bath he has every morning. I have a cold one which I equally enjoy.--

Hester called on Emily again today, & found her very queerly dressed. I have not seen her yet. I suppose she will call on me soon, but somehow I fancy she is not very anxious to come in my way. And indeed if she tells any of her falsehoods

in my presence, I shall be scarcely able to repress any indignation, & should almost be inclined to give her such a dressing as she never had yet. Seriously speaking I certainly never wish to come into dispute with her, being thoroughly persuaded that she is beyond mending.
I get much stronger, & go about in a chair, besides walking a little. No riding weather yet. Hester & I are very happy together,
